source_of_truth: cargo bench -p synapse-action --bench action_software_press -- --quiet --noplot stdout plus target/criterion/action_software_press/new/estimates.json

cases.after: new_events:2 first_event:KeyDown { key: Key { code: Named { value: "f12" }, use_scancode: false } } last_event:KeyUp { key: Key { code: Named { value: "f12" }, use_scancode: false } } actor_empty:true

cases.before: snapshot:ActionStateSnapshot { held_keys: [], held_key_bits: [], held_key_timer_keys: [], held_key_timer_count: 0, held_buttons: [], held_button_bits: [], pad_state: {} }
cases.after: error_code:ACTION_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE snapshot:ActionStateSnapshot { held_keys: [], held_key_bits: [], held_key_timer_keys: [], held_key_timer_count: 0, held_buttons: [], held_button_bits: [], pad_state: {} } final_snapshot:ActionStateSnapshot { held_keys: [], held_key_bits: [], held_key_timer_keys: [], held_key_timer_count: 0, held_buttons: [], held_button_bits: [], pad_state: {} }

notes: Criterion baseline is the WSL RecordingBackend actor path, which is intentionally not used to enforce the Windows SendInput p99 target. The bench contains an opt-in native Windows path gated by SYNAPSE_ACTION_SOFTWARE_PRESS_REAL=1; that path reads GetAsyncKeyState(F12) after KeyDown ack and after KeyUp cleanup, then enforces p99 <= 3000000 ns on the Win11 reference machine.
